symbols: arrows piercing the body — persecution and suffering for the faith
bound figure — captivity under hostile powers
description: A Tintoretto painting of Saint Sebastian bound and pierced with arrows, depicted with Venetian dramatic lighting and rich color. The saint's muscular body is rendered with anatomical precision against a dark background.
theological_description: Saint Sebastian's martyrdom embodies Christ's promise: 'You will be hated by all for my name's sake. But the one who endures to the end will be saved' (MAT.10.22). His endurance reflects Paul's confidence: 'I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed' (ROM.8.18).

scripture_refs.reason: Present sufferings not comparing with future glory
Be faithful unto death and receive the crown of life
